title: Draft quantum order tasks many agencies with reinvigorating the tech’s development - Nextgov/FCW
why:
- A new executive order is being developed to establish a whole-of-government approach to strengthen U.S. quantum information sciences and technology.
- The order tasks major agencies—including the Office of Science and Technology Policy, Departments of Energy, Defense, and Commerce—with updating the National Quantum Strategy within 180 days of its signing.
- A national effort will be created to achieve a quantum computer for scientific applications and discovery (QCSAD), with at least one system to be housed at a Department of Energy facility.
- The Department of Energy is directed to work with private sector partners to expedite access to quantum computers for the scientific community, while Commerce will develop plans to continue investing in commercial quantum companies.
- The order prioritizes advancements in quantum sensors and quantum networking, with agencies required to submit five-year roadmaps for expanding these technologies.
- The executive order emphasizes workforce development by expanding quantum education and training opportunities and tasks NSF and the Department of Labor with tracking workforce development progress.
